title: Bernard Arnault's business-first strategy with foreign heads of state, from Trump to Putin
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (3/6). The head of LVMH, which generates 70% of its revenue outside Europe, has fostered relationships with the leaders of the markets he targets. Foremost among them is the president of the United States, whom he has courted since the 1980s.

title: French MPs pass law banning social media for under-15s
Why 19 men have been charged in a French mass rapes case
Zelensky sacks army chief Syrsky after days of protests
subhead: Volodymyr Zelensky announced he was appointing joint forces commander Mykhailo Drapaty in his place. Questions over Oleksandr Syrsky's future emerged last week, when Zelensky removed highly popular defense minister Mykhailo Fedorov just six months after appointing him.

title: OpenAI says its AI technology acted on its own in an 'unprecedented' hack of another company
subhead: On Tuesday, OpenAI released a statement revealing that its artificial intelligence system had hacked the AI startup Hugging Face, describing the breach as a 'significant security incident.' According to OpenAI, its AI models used stolen credentials and went to 'extreme lengths' to access secret information.

title: New monkey species identified in Lomami National Park, Democratic Republic of Congo
subhead: First photographed in 2008, this small colobus monkey with pink-orange lips has now been officially described by an international team of researchers – and may be immediately classified as 'endangered.'

title: Orléans museum launches 'Wanted' campaign to track down 424 looted artworks
subhead: Nearly 85 years after Nazi forces looted its collections, the Orléans Museum of Fine Arts is turning to auctioneers, notaries and collectors to help recover the paintings and drawings still missing.

Iran bets on war of attrition against the US
subhead: As US airstrikes persist in Iran, Iranian authorities are, for now, avoiding a full-scale confrontation. Their strategy is to leverage the threat of global energy disruption to force Washington to reconsider its approach.

title: 20 men, a 'sperm party,' and two days of sexual torment: The brutality of the 'libertine' rapes case
subhead: Over the course of a long weekend, one of Christophe B.'s former partners was forced to endure sexual assaults by numerous men at their home. Some of these assaults were broadcast live on the swinging website Wyylde.
byline: Jérôme Lefilliâtre and  Lorraine de Foucher

title: Far-right Nigel Farage faces an unlikely opponent: Count Binface
subhead: The leader of the far-right party resigned his seat as MP in an attempt to distract from investigations into his party's donors, but he now faces an unlikely opponent: Count Binface.

title: China's AI doctrine is a challenge for Europe
